slides.title: The Gatekeeper 💂
slides.content:
The `if` statement allows code to run **only if** a condition is true.

It's like a gatekeeper: "If you have the password, you can enter."

quizData.question: When does the code inside an 'if' block run?

options.text: Only if the condition is true

quizData.explanation: It executes only when the condition evaluates to `true`.

slides.title: Syntax 🧱
slides.content: Put the condition in parentheses `()` and the code to run inside curly braces `{}`.

slides.title: Real Example 🎟️
Curly Braces {}
slides.content:
## Block of Code

The code inside `{ }` is called a **block**.

Indenting the code inside comparisons makes it easier to read!
